mnemonics: JFrame First: Just Frame Really Anchors Main (JFrame is the main container that must be created first in a Swing app)
Event Dispatch Thread: Every Visual Thing Demands Respect (EDT is the single thread responsible for handling all GUI events and painting)
keyPoints: Every Swing application must start by creating and making a JFrame visible.
All GUI creation and updates must be performed on the Event Dispatch Thread (EDT) using SwingUtilities.invokeLater().
Components (like JButton, JLabel) are added to the JFrame's content pane, not directly to the JFrame itself.
commonMistakes: Forgetting to call setVisible(true) on the JFrame, making the application window invisible.
Updating the GUI from a background thread instead of the EDT, causing unpredictable behavior and crashes.
Adding components directly to the JFrame instead of to its content pane (e.g., frame.add(comp) instead of frame.getContentPane().add(comp)).
